title: Boat Rental in Muskoka Lakes: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
The Muskoka Lakes, a network of interconnected lakes including Lake Muskoka, Lake Rosseau, and Lake Joseph, offer some of Ontario's finest boating. Renting a boat here need not break the bank; prices for a half-day small motorboat start around 250 CAD. BoatHire24 connects you with local owners for a straightforward booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Muskoka Lakes?

Prices range from 80 CAD for a shared tour to over 2,000 CAD for a full-day crewed yacht. A small motorboat for half a day typically costs 250, 400 CAD, while a catamaran full-day rental averages 1,200, 1,800 CAD. For a tailored experience, consider a yacht charter in Muskoka Lakes on BoatHire24, where you can compare verified boat listings and choose what suits your budget.

  • Shared sightseeing tour: 80, 150 CAD per person
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): 250, 400 CAD total
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): 1,200, 1,800 CAD total
  • Crewed yacht full-day (8 hours): 1,500, 2,500 CAD total

Where to set off in Muskoka Lakes?

The region offers numerous public docks and marinas. In Lake Muskoka, start from Bracebridge Bay Marina or Gravenhurst Wharf. On Lake Rosseau, the Windermere Basin is a popular departure point, and Port Carling provides access to the locks connecting the lakes. Lake Joseph has several quiet launch ramps near the Joseph River. Each location offers easy parking and amenities.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Motorboat (bowrider or deck boat), Ideal for families exploring coves; holds up to 8 guests; from 300 CAD half-day.
  2. Pontoon boat, Perfect for relaxed cruises and swimming stops; holds up to 12 guests; from 350 CAD half-day.
  3. Sailboat (e.g. a 26-foot sloop), Great for a quiet day on the water with a small crew; holds up to 4 guests; from 400 CAD half-day.
  4. Catamaran (e.g. a 38-foot Lagoon), Spacious and stable, ideal for groups; holds up to 12 guests; from 1,200 CAD full-day.

For a stylish option, browse the Salty Sun Charters charter page, which features a well-maintained motor yacht suited for day trips with a skipper.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Muskoka Lakes?

In Ontario, operators of powered boats must hold a valid Pleasure Craft Operator Card (PCOC). Renters are responsible for having the correct licence; the boat owner will confirm exact requirements before departure. For those without a licence, crewed charters are the simplest option.

Visit the Muskoka Lakes Museum in Gravenhurst to learn about the region's steamboat history before you set off, it gives context to the waters you will explore.

At a glance

  • Price band: 80, 2,500 CAD
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: Canadian Dollar (CAD)
  • Licence required: PCOC for powered boats

Questions about boat rental in Muskoka Lakes

Can I rent a boat in Muskoka Lakes without a licence?

Yes, but only if you hire a crewed charter where a licensed skipper is included in the rental fee.

What is the best time of year for boating in Muskoka Lakes?

The high season runs from late June to early September, when water temperatures are warmest and weather is most reliable.

How long does a typical boat rental last?

Most rentals offer half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours) options; some owners also provide sunset cruises of 2, 3 hours.

Are there any restrictions on where I can take the boat?

Yes, renters must stay within the lake on which the boat is based unless prior agreement is reached with the owner.

Whether you are planning a family outing or a luxury escape, renting a boat in Muskoka Lakes in 2026 is straightforward with the BoatHire24 marketplace. If you own a boat in the region, you can list your vessel and earn income from holidaymakers looking to explore the lakes. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Muskoka Lakes and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
